stories.title: Trump confirms calling Netanyahu 'crazy' in call over Lebanon strikes
stories.description: The president said he was "a little bit perturbed" by Israel's operations against Hezbollah, which he says are undermining U.S.-Iran peace talks.

stories.title: CENTCOM calls Iran's claim of striking US destroyer a lie
stories.description: Iran said it hit a US Navy destroyer's command center in the Gulf of Oman; CENTCOM said the claim is false.

stories.title: Kim Jong Un calls for 'exponential' nuclear expansion
stories.description: North Korea's leader toured a newly operational nuclear material production facility, saying output capacity has more than doubled in five years.

stories.title: Israel, Lebanon agree to ceasefire contingent on Hezbollah withdrawal
stories.description: The US-brokered deal, reached after two days of talks in Washington, calls for pilot zones where Lebanese forces would replace Hezbollah in the south.

stories.title: Trump strips job protections from 8,000 federal workers
stories.description: An executive order signed Wednesday converts senior career employees to at-will status, removing their right to appeal disciplinary actions or terminations.

stories.title: Iran, US working on 'final formula' to end war, Araghchi says
stories.description: Iran's foreign minister said both sides are reviewing exchanged texts, though he cautioned that no progress has been made in formal negotiations.

stories.title: Germany, France, UK sketch plan to engage Putin on Ukraine
stories.description: The three European allies see a shift in momentum favoring Kyiv as U.S.-mediated talks remain stalled since February.

stories.title: House passes war powers resolution to end Iran war
stories.description: Four Republicans joined Democrats in the 215-208 vote, delivering a bipartisan rebuke to President Trump over the three-month-old conflict.

stories.title: Rubio says Greenland is part of Denmark 'for now'
stories.description: The secretary of state told lawmakers that U.S. talks with Denmark and Greenland on collective defense are "in a good place."

stories.title: Proactiv, Dr. Pimple Popper acne creams had benzene 15 times FDA limit
stories.description: Bloomberg reported that government lab results showed recalled acne products from Proactiv and SLMD contained benzene levels far exceeding federal safety limits.

stories.title: Pentagon watchdog to lead oversight of Iran war
stories.description: Inspector General Platte B. Moring III will coordinate reviews across the Pentagon, State Department, and USAID for Operation Epic Fury.

stories.title: Trump confirms he will attend G7 summit in France
stories.description: The president said on Truth Social he will travel to Évian-les-Bains after a UFC fight at the White House on June 14, his 80th birthday.

stories.title: Bass, Pratt advance to LA mayoral runoff after Tuesday primary
stories.description: Early results show the incumbent mayor leading with roughly 37%, followed by reality TV star Spencer Pratt, who edged out Nithya Raman for second place.

stories.title: Vanguard's VOO becomes first ETF to hit $1 trillion
stories.description: The S&P 500 tracker crossed the milestone after averaging more than $1 billion in daily inflows, according to the Financial Times.

stories.title: Trump administration appeals order to refund $166B in tariffs
stories.description: The DOJ argues the trade court cannot mandate universal refunds, potentially forcing importers who haven't filed individual lawsuits to sue for their money back.

stories.title: California primary sets November matchups across key races
stories.description: Wiener and Chan will compete for Pelosi's former House seat, while Bass heads to an L.A. mayoral runoff and the governor's race remains too close to call.

stories.title: Ukraine hits St. Petersburg oil terminal as Putin's economic forum opens
stories.description: Zelenskyy confirmed overnight drone strikes on the oil terminal, a naval base, and a weapons facility, targeting sites over 1,100 kilometers from Ukraine's border.

stories.title: Trump says he and Iran's Khamenei 'getting along quite well,' eyes meeting
stories.description: The president reiterated that Tehran agreed to forgo nuclear weapons and said a face-to-face meeting with the supreme leader is likely.

stories.title: Iran drone strike hits Kuwait airport, halting flights
stories.description: Iranian drones and missiles struck Terminal 1 at Kuwait International Airport on Wednesday, injuring several and forcing a full suspension of commercial flights.

stories.title: US proposes tariffs on 60 economies over forced labor
stories.description: The sweeping Section 301 action covers more than 99% of U.S. imports and marks a major step in rebuilding tariff authority after the Supreme Court struck down IEEPA levies.

stories.title: Iowa farmer Lahn defeats Trump-backed Feenstra in GOP governor primary
stories.description: Zach Lahn, aligned with RFK Jr.'s MAHA movement, edged Rep. Randy Feenstra by less than a point in Tuesday's five-candidate race.

stories.title: SpaceX plans to price record IPO at $135 a share
stories.description: The rocket and satellite company aims to raise $75 billion in the largest public offering ever, more than doubling Saudi Aramco's record.

stories.title: Bolivia's defense minister resigns amid weeks of mass protests
stories.description: Marcelo Salinas's departure is the highest-level exit from President Rodrigo Paz's government since unrest erupted in early May.

stories.title: Trump calls Vance-Rubio 2028 ticket 'unbeatable'
stories.description: The president's podcast remarks are his most direct praise yet of the pairing, though he still stopped short of a formal endorsement.

stories.title: CBS fires Scott Pelley after clash with new '60 Minutes' boss
stories.description: The veteran correspondent was terminated Tuesday after accusing Bari Weiss of "murdering" the iconic newsmagazine during a staff meeting.

stories.title: Supreme Court lets Alabama eliminate a Black-majority district
stories.description: The 6-3 ruling allows a GOP-drawn congressional map for the 2026 midterms, overriding a lower court finding of intentional racial discrimination.

stories.title: Dr. Oz helms White House briefing, dodges non-health questions
stories.description: The CMS administrator announced 160 new drugs on the TrumpRx discount platform while deflecting questions outside his portfolio.

stories.title: Jeffries says Democrats not focused on Trump impeachment
stories.description: The House minority leader told business leaders the party will prioritize affordability and anti-corruption ahead of the November midterms.

stories.title: George Santos faces federal probe over Kalshi bets on his own SOTU attendance
stories.description: The DOJ and CFTC are investigating whether the disgraced former congressman profited by wagering against his own appearance at the State of the Union.

stories.title: Trump endorses Colombia presidential candidate De La Espriella
stories.description: The endorsement came via Truth Social on Tuesday as the conservative outsider prepares to face leftist senator Ivan Cepeda in a June 21 runoff.

stories.title: IRGC attacks US Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain
stories.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards launched missile and drone attacks on the U.S. naval base and a regional airbase, escalating tit-for-tat exchanges.

stories.title: Navy veteran Bennett wins NJ-7 primary to face absent Rep. Kean
stories.description: Bennett will challenge Rep. Tom Kean Jr., who has missed over 100 congressional votes during a months-long undisclosed medical absence.

stories.title: Senate confirms first 'not qualified' judge of Trump's second term
stories.description: Katie Lane was approved 52-46 for a Montana federal judgeship despite the ABA finding she had never tried a case as lead counsel.

stories.title: Rubio says Netanyahu's plan to seize 70% of Gaza contradicts US vision
stories.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio told lawmakers Tuesday that Israel's expansion directive is not part of Washington's plan for the territory.

stories.title: UNICEF says Iran war delaying lifesaving aid for children
stories.description: The agency warned Tuesday that surging transport costs and Gulf shipping disruptions are forcing it to rely on costly air freight to reach children.

stories.title: Oz calls Trump's health 'spectacular' after latest checkup
stories.description: The CMS administrator dismissed concerns about Trump's frequent Walter Reed visits, saying the president keeps returning because he "likes the results."

stories.title: US sanctions two rebel commanders over eastern Congo conflict
stories.description: The Treasury Department targeted an M23 intelligence chief and an FDLR special operations commander fueling violence in the DRC.

stories.title: Kremlin demands Ukraine cede territory hours after deadly mass attack
stories.description: Russia launched 73 missiles and 656 drones at Ukrainian cities, killing at least 22, before demanding Kyiv withdraw from occupied regions.

stories.title: Pentagon hires convicted Jan. 6 rioter for counterterrorism role
stories.description: Elias Irizarry, who pleaded guilty to entering the Capitol during the Jan. 6 attack, was appointed to the Defense Department's irregular warfare office.

stories.title: Bahrain bans citizens from traveling to Iran and Iraq
stories.description: The ban, citing security tensions, comes as millions of Shiites prepare for pilgrimage season, drawing accusations of sectarian discrimination.

stories.title: Rubio says five countries may take stranded Afghan allies
stories.description: More than 1,000 Afghans who aided U.S. forces remain stuck at a base in Qatar, but Rubio told senators they won't be resettled in America.

stories.title: Platner meets with Senate Democrats amid sexting scandal
stories.description: The Maine Democratic frontrunner visited Washington for fundraisers and face-to-face meetings as senators weigh his viability one week before the primary.

stories.title: Blanche confirms end to $1.8B anti-weaponization amid backlash
stories.description: Acting AG Todd Blanche told Congress the administration will not move forward with the controversial taxpayer-funded program.

stories.title: Hegseth blocks Navy officers from admiral promotions
stories.description: The defense secretary removed at least seven captains from a one-star admiral list, including two women and two Black men, reports say.

stories.title: Takaichi urges Iran to restore free passage through Hormuz
stories.description: Japan's PM called on Iran's president to show flexibility in U.S.-Iran talks and reopen the strait to all vessels.

stories.title: Trump audit immunity remains unchallenged after anti-weaponization fund pause
stories.description: The audit shield blocking IRS probes of Trump's past tax filings faces no direct legal challenge, even as a linked $1.8 billion fund is frozen.

stories.title: Hundreds protest US Ebola quarantine facility in Kenya
stories.description: Demonstrators in Nanyuki demanded the closure of a planned 50-bed facility at Laikipia Air Base days after a High Court order halted the project.

stories.title: Russia launches major strike on Kyiv, setting apartment buildings ablaze
stories.description: Moscow followed through on its threat of "systematic strikes," hitting a 24-story residential building and sparking fires across Ukraine's capital early Tuesday.

stories.title: White House Correspondents' Dinner set for July 24 after shooting postponement
stories.description: The WHCA announced the rescheduled date Tuesday, nearly three months after a gunman disrupted the original April 25 event at the Washington Hilton.

stories.title: State Dept. to cut Africa visa-processing sites from 50 to 20 hubs
stories.description: Secretary of State Rubio approved a directive consolidating consular operations across Africa, forcing applicants in non-hub countries to travel abroad for visas.

stories.title: Frederiksen secures third term as Denmark's PM with new centre-left coalition
stories.description: The deal ends over two months of deadlocked talks following March's fragmented snap election, which saw 12 parties win seats in parliament.

stories.title: Trump signs scaled-back AI executive order with 30-day model review
stories.description: The executive order asks AI companies to voluntarily submit powerful models to federal review before release, down from an earlier 90-day proposal.

stories.title: Pentagon bars journalists from press office, declaring it classified space
stories.description: The designation marks the latest escalation in a yearlong campaign to restrict media access to the Defense Department.

stories.title: EU climate chief says COP summits falling short of science
stories.description: Wopke Hoekstra's remarks come a week before the Bonn SB64 conference, seen as a key test of multilateral climate commitments.

stories.title: Former FBI officials launch support network for agents under Patel
stories.description: The group offers legal, mental health, and career assistance to employees facing what organizers call politically motivated firings and intimidation.

stories.title: Appeals court rules Pentagon transgender troop ban illegal
stories.description: A divided D.C. Circuit panel ruled the Pentagon's transgender military ban illegal but allowed the military to keep barring new enlistees.

stories.title: Iran cuts off U.S. talks, vows to fully block Hormuz
stories.description: Tehran says it will completely shut the Strait of Hormuz and halt all negotiations until Israel withdraws from Lebanon, according to state media.

stories.title: Anthropic confidentially files for IPO after $965B valuation
stories.description: The Claude AI maker submitted a draft S-1 to the SEC on Monday, joining SpaceX and OpenAI in a historic wave of tech IPOs expected this year.

stories.title: Florida sues OpenAI and Sam Altman over AI safety
stories.description: The state becomes the first to sue OpenAI, alleging the company prioritized profit over safety in marketing ChatGPT to users.

stories.title: Armenia's Pashinyan rejects Russia's call for EU referendum
stories.description: The Armenian prime minister pushed back on Monday as Moscow escalated pressure over Yerevan's ties with the European Union ahead of June 7 elections.

stories.title: Nvidia unveils RTX Spark, its first Windows PC chip in over a decade
stories.description: CEO Jensen Huang announced the Arm-based processor at Computex, calling it "the most efficient PC chip ever built."

stories.title: CERN finds high-significance hint of physics beyond the Standard Model
stories.description: Rare B meson decays measured at the LHC deviate from predictions at four sigma, giving physicists a one-in-16,000 chance of a statistical fluke.

stories.title: Blast at explosives storage site kills dozens in Myanmar
stories.description: At least 46 people died and about 70 were injured after an explosion at a mining explosives depot in Shan State on Sunday.

stories.title: Trump tours ballroom site, says inauguration will be held there
stories.description: During a Fox News interview with Lara Trump, the president revealed plans to host the next inauguration in the new White House ballroom.

stories.title: France detains 780 after deadly riots follow PSG's Champions League win
stories.description: One person was found dead and 219 injured as celebrations of PSG's second straight title devolved into mass disorder across 15 French cities.

stories.title: Taiwan vows not to be 'silenced' after China expels NYT reporter
stories.description: The presidential office condemned Beijing after China expelled a New York Times correspondent over a Taiwan president interview.

stories.title: Trump signs order to narrow childhood vaccine schedule
stories.description: The executive order directs the CDC to align with an HHS assessment recommending vaccines for 11 diseases instead of the current 17.

stories.title: Hegseth denies Iran war delayed $14B Taiwan arms sale
stories.description: Speaking at the Shangri-La Dialogue, the defense secretary contradicted a Navy official who said the deal was paused to conserve munitions.

stories.title: Lula rejects U.S. terrorist label for Brazil's largest gangs
stories.description: Brazil's president called the designation "disappointing" and accused Washington of treating Brazil "like a banana republic."

stories.title: Philippines says China threat persists despite Trump-Xi summit
stories.description: Defense Secretary Teodoro said at the Shangri-La Dialogue that the recent U.S.-China diplomatic thaw has not eased South China Sea tensions.

stories.title: Hegseth says China 'credibly preparing' for military action in Asia
stories.description: The defense secretary spoke Saturday at the Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore as China's defense minister skipped the summit for a second year.

stories.title: Quarter of trapped oil tankers slip out of Hormuz
stories.description: Twenty-nine of 109 large tankers stranded since the Iran war began have crossed the strait, but Chevron's CEO says vessels are still being attacked.

stories.title: Federal judge presses DOJ to justify $100,000 H-1B fee
stories.description: Judge Leo Sorokin questioned the limits of presidential power to impose the fee, posing pointed hypotheticals to DOJ lawyers during a Friday hearing.

stories.title: Rubio thanks Pakistan for Iran mediation efforts
stories.description: Secretary of State Rubio met with Pakistani Foreign Minister Dar on Friday, praising Islamabad's role in mediating the U.S.-Iran conflict.

stories.title: Bondi acknowledges redaction errors in Epstein files testimony
stories.description: The former attorney general defended DOJ's overall handling of the files but refused to apologize for failures that exposed survivors' identities.

stories.title: 'Gang of Six' GOP senators threaten Trump's agenda
stories.description: Six Republican senators have emerged as a bloc capable of derailing Trump's priorities in the narrowly divided Senate.

stories.title: Top US general meets Cuban military leaders at Guantanamo Bay
stories.description: Gen. Francis Donovan held rare talks with senior Cuban officers at the base perimeter Friday amid a sharp escalation in U.S.-Cuba tensions.

stories.title: US-Iran draft deal includes $300B reconstruction fund for Tehran
stories.description: Gulf states and China would finance the program, contingent on a final peace agreement ending the three-month war.

stories.title: Judge reopens Trump's $10B IRS lawsuit over fraud claims
stories.description: U.S. District Judge Kathleen Williams ordered Trump's legal team to address allegations that the settlement with DOJ was "premised on deception."

stories.title: China building 80-plus launch pads near nuclear silos
stories.description: Reuters satellite imagery reveals a sprawling network of launch stations, bunkers, and communications hubs in Xinjiang designed to bolster Beijing's second-strike capability.

stories.title: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket explodes during test at Cape Canaveral
stories.description: The blast Thursday evening shook nearby homes but caused no injuries, dealing a fresh blow to Jeff Bezos' rocket program weeks before a planned launch.

stories.title: Judge strikes down New Hampshire's proof-of-citizenship voting law
stories.description: The ruling immediately restores sworn affidavits as an option for new voters who lack a birth certificate or passport to prove citizenship.

stories.title: Bessent signals phased Iran sanctions relief tied to deal benchmarks
stories.description: Trump weighs a tentative 60-day agreement as the Treasury secretary says any rollback would come slowly and only after Tehran meets key demands.

stories.title: Hungary's Magyar vows 'reliable' NATO role but won't arm Ukraine
stories.description: Prime Minister Magyar met NATO chief Rutte in Brussels to discuss the Ankara summit and pledged renewed alliance cooperation.

stories.title: Japan calls Russian criticism of its military buildup 'ridiculous' at UN
stories.description: Tokyo's UN ambassador pushed back after Russia accused Japan and Germany of dangerous remilitarization at a Security Council debate.

stories.title: Democrats threaten subpoenas after Bondi dodges Trump questions in Epstein hearing
stories.description: Bondi acknowledged redaction errors but refused to discuss Trump, shifting responsibility to Acting AG Todd Blanche.

stories.title: US denies Iran's claim it destroyed American aircraft in Bushehr
stories.description: The unverified assertion, broadcast on Iranian state TV, comes as both sides reached a preliminary deal to extend their ceasefire by 60 days.

stories.title: Hochul signs NYC pied-à-terre tax into law as part of $269B budget
stories.description: The new surcharge on luxury second homes, set to take effect July 1, is expected to generate $500 million annually for New York City.

stories.title: Talarico says he raised $3M in a day after Paxton's Texas runoff win
stories.description: The Democratic Senate nominee's record haul highlights party enthusiasm for flipping a Texas seat held by Republicans since 1993.

stories.title: Bari Weiss shakes up '60 Minutes' with new producer, ousted correspondents
stories.description: CBS News editor-in-chief replaced the executive producer and dismissed multiple correspondents in one of the program's most dramatic restructurings.

stories.title: Supreme Court sides with Black death row inmate on jury bias
stories.description: In a 5-4 ruling, the justices found Mississippi courts wrongly concluded Terry Pitchford waived his right to challenge racial discrimination in jury selection.

stories.title: Whitmer says she won't run for president in 2028
stories.description: The Michigan governor ended months of speculation at the Mackinac Policy Conference, saying a "robust group" will run but she won't be among them.

stories.title: Israel strikes Beirut suburb targeting Iranian militia missile commander
stories.description: The Choueifat attack, the first near the capital in weeks, came one day before new Pentagon security talks between Israel and Lebanon.

stories.title: NYC Mayor Mamdani launches charter Commission on Government Efficiency
stories.description: The Commission on Government Efficiency will review the city charter and bring proposed amendments to voters in November.

stories.title: Trump refiles $10B defamation suit against WSJ over Epstein report
stories.description: The amended complaint, filed Tuesday in Miami federal court, names Rupert Murdoch, News Corp, and two Journal reporters as defendants.

stories.title: April PCE inflation hits 3-year high as Fed's Warsh faces first price test
stories.description: The headline PCE index rose 3.8% year-over-year while core PCE came in at 3.3%, marking the steepest inflation since 2023.

stories.title: Tesla's Optimus robot factory raises first steel at Giga Texas
stories.description: Drone footage shows vertical steel framing at the Austin site designed for up to 10 million humanoid robots per year.

stories.title: Judge declines to block Trump mail-in voting order
stories.description: Democrats' challenge was premature because no agency has acted on the directive yet, the court ruled.

stories.title: US sanctions Iran's Hormuz toll authority, cites IRGC ties
stories.description: Treasury designated the Persian Gulf Strait Authority, the body collecting transit fees from ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Ex-CIA officer charged after FBI finds 303 gold bars in his home
stories.description: David Rush allegedly checked out tens of millions in gold from the CIA for "work expenses" and kept it, authorities say.

stories.title: Trump administration in talks to fund US drone companies
stories.description: Unusual Machines, linked to Donald Trump Jr., and other manufacturers are in negotiations with the Pentagon for production financing, the Wall Street Journal reported.

stories.title: Israel declares 2,000 sq km of southern Lebanon a combat zone
stories.description: The IDF warned residents to evacuate north of the Zahrani River as it prepares expanded operations against Hezbollah despite an April ceasefire.

stories.title: US re-adds UN expert Albanese to sanctions list
stories.description: The Treasury Department restored the designation Wednesday after an appeals court stayed a lower court order that had blocked the measures.

stories.title: Park entrance fees fund Trump's D.C. projects, Times finds
stories.description: At least $67 million in national park visitor fees is being spent on fountains and the Reflecting Pool renovation in Washington.

stories.title: EU sets June 16 date to open Ukraine accession talks
stories.description: The European Commission plans to propose opening the first cluster of membership negotiations with Ukraine and Moldova, ending a two-year deadlock.

stories.title: DHS Secretary Mullin dismisses Newark hunger strike, blames Kim for pepper-spray clash
stories.description: Mullin called the protest a dispute over "ethnic food" at a Cabinet meeting, as Sen. Booker visited Delaney Hall and called it "a moral stain."

stories.title: Trump says Iran 'negotiating on fumes,' midterms won't alter war strategy
stories.description: During a Cabinet meeting, the president insisted electoral pressures would not soften his stance as peace talks with Tehran remain uncertain.

stories.title: US needs years to rebuild weapons used in Iran war, CSIS finds
stories.description: A new analysis warns that restocking Tomahawk missiles, Patriot interceptors, and THAAD interceptors could take until 2030, raising concerns about readiness against China.

stories.title: North Korea claims first use of AI in missile tests
stories.description: Kim Jong Un supervised Tuesday's tests of AI-guided cruise missiles, nuclear-capable ballistic warheads, and precision artillery rockets, KCNA reported.

stories.title: Biden sues DOJ to block release of ghostwriter audio
stories.description: The Trump DOJ reversed its position and plans to release roughly 70 hours of recordings to Congress and the Heritage Foundation by June 15.

stories.title: Cuba calls US oil blockade 'an act of war' at UN Security Council
stories.description: Foreign Minister Bruno Rodríguez Parrilla urged the international community to mobilize against what he called an unfolding humanitarian catastrophe.

stories.title: UN Security Council condemns drone strike on UAE nuclear plant
stories.description: The council unanimously called the May 17 attack on Barakah a violation of international law but did not assign blame.

stories.title: Trump's Board of Peace fund sits empty despite $17B in pledges
stories.description: The World Bank-managed reconstruction fund for Gaza has received no donor money four months after launch, the Financial Times reported.

stories.title: DOJ sues UCLA over antisemitism in third suit against UC this year
stories.description: The lawsuit alleges the university was "deliberately indifferent" to harassment of Jewish and Israeli students during a 2024 pro-Palestinian encampment.

stories.title: Iran's judiciary blocks presidential order to restore internet
stories.description: President Pezeshkian ordered international internet access restored after a near-90-day blackout, but the judiciary suspended the body tasked with carrying it out.

stories.title: Trump moves Cabinet meeting to White House as Iran deal hangs in balance
stories.description: The president cited weather concerns for shifting a rare full Cabinet retreat from Camp David, with fragile US-Iran negotiations set to dominate the agenda.

stories.title: Supreme Court rejects Florida's bid to sue over immigrant truck licenses
stories.description: The justices declined to hear the state-versus-state dispute over commercial driver's licenses issued to undocumented immigrants.

stories.title: Zelensky says US missile defense talks have stalled
stories.description: Ukraine's president declared air defense Kyiv's top priority and said he is accelerating efforts to produce anti-ballistic systems in Europe instead.

stories.title: SPLC asks judge to toss DOJ indictment, alleging vindictive prosecution
stories.description: Lawyers argue the 11-count case is the "culmination of a top-down, retributive campaign" directed by President Trump against political enemies.

stories.title: Cuba publishes names of 2,000 pardoned prisoners
stories.description: The full list, released Monday in the official gazette, enables rights groups to verify whether any political detainees were included in the April amnesty.

stories.title: Putin signs decree canceling debts for new Ukraine war recruits
stories.description: The Kremlin is offering up to 10 million roubles in debt relief to those who enlist for at least one year, along with their spouses.

stories.title: Rubio aide Needham promoted to deputy national security adviser
stories.description: The move deepens Rubio's hold on national security policy as he continues serving as both secretary of state and acting NSA.

stories.title: Iran says Khamenei suffered only 'superficial' wounds in February airstrikes
stories.description: Health Ministry officials described the supreme leader's injuries as minor, contradicting months of reports that he was in a coma or lost a leg.

stories.title: Carney calls Alberta separation vote a 'dangerous bluff,' cites Brexit experience
stories.description: The prime minister drew on his time leading the Bank of England during Brexit to warn Albertans against treating the October referendum as leverage.

stories.title: South Carolina Senate kills Trump-backed redistricting plan
stories.description: Record early voting turnout helped sway Republicans to reject new congressional maps targeting Rep. Jim Clyburn's district.

stories.title: Massie files for 2028 race days after Trump-backed primary defeat
stories.description: The Kentucky congressman declined to rule out a presidential bid, saying he hasn't decided which office to seek.

stories.title: AI safety filters on Meta, Google models stripped in minutes, FT finds
stories.description: Freely available software tools can remove guardrails from major AI models, enabling responses on bioweapons, malware, and exploitation.

stories.title: Trump administration proposes NDAs for all federal workers
stories.description: OPM published a draft nondisclosure agreement in the Federal Register on Tuesday, targeting leaks of internal government information to the media.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV calls to 'disarm' AI in first encyclical
stories.description: "Magnifica Humanitas" warns against an unchecked technology race and declares just war theory "outdated" in the age of autonomous weapons.

stories.title: Oil and LNG tankers resume Strait of Hormuz transits after three-month halt
stories.description: Limited commercial shipping is restarting through the critical chokepoint, though volumes remain far below pre-war levels.

stories.title: Supreme Court backs Trump on immigration judge speech curbs
stories.description: The justices reversed a Fourth Circuit ruling on procedural grounds without addressing whether the speech restrictions are constitutional.

stories.title: Inaudible sounds hidden in podcasts can hijack AI voice assistants, researchers find
stories.description: Researchers demonstrated that imperceptible audio embedded in everyday media can trick voice AI into unauthorized actions like sending emails or downloading files.

stories.title: NATO weighs return to Iraq after wartime evacuation
stories.description: The alliance's advisory mission was fully relocated to Italy in March after Iranian strikes on bases in northern Iraq.

stories.title: DOJ cites White House shooting to push for ballroom project
stories.description: The Justice Department filed a brief Sunday invoking Saturday's fatal shooting near the White House to argue for lifting the injunction on Trump's $400 million ballroom.

stories.title: Iranian hackers posed as recruiters to target US aviation engineers
stories.description: Palo Alto Networks' Unit 42 uncovered a campaign using fake job ads and compromised video tools to infiltrate aviation and energy networks.

stories.title: US naval blockade of Iran hits 100 redirected ships
stories.description: CENTCOM announced Friday that forces have redirected more than 100 commercial vessels since the blockade of Iranian ports began April 13.

stories.title: Trump urges Arab leaders to normalize Israel ties as Iran deal nears
stories.description: Arab and Muslim leaders were silent when Trump pressed them to join the Abraham Accords during a call about the pending Iran deal.

stories.title: Zelensky confirms strike on chemical plant 1,700 km inside Russia
stories.description: Ukraine's overnight drone campaign hit a Perm Krai chemical plant and a Novorossiysk oil terminal, with Zelensky saying production has been "completely halted."

stories.title: Zelensky calls Germany's associate EU membership plan 'unfair'
stories.description: Ukraine's president told EU leaders the proposal would leave Kyiv "voiceless" and urged full membership instead.

stories.title: Taiwan says China deployed 100-plus vessels after Trump-Xi summit
stories.description: The fleet spans from the Yellow Sea to the South China Sea and western Pacific, marking one of Beijing's largest maritime shows of force in years.

stories.title: Democrats demand Rubio explain visa for fugitive Polish minister
stories.description: House Democrats called the expedited visa for wanted ex-minister Zbigniew Ziobro "a massive abuse of power" and demanded answers by June 21.

stories.title: SpaceX launches first Starship V3 but loses booster over Gulf of Mexico
stories.description: The 12th test flight debuted SpaceX's most powerful rocket design, but engine failures sent the Super Heavy booster crashing into the Gulf of Mexico.

stories.title: DOJ deletes press releases on Jan. 6 charges from its website
stories.description: NBC News found the vast majority of news releases about Jan. 6 defendants were removed, as a $1.776 billion compensation fund draws bipartisan scrutiny.

stories.title: Iran condemns US sanctions on its envoy to Lebanon
stories.description: Tehran called the Treasury Department's designation of ambassador Mohammad Reza Sheibani "illegal," accusing Washington of violating international law.

stories.title: Judge tosses Abrego Garcia smuggling charges as vindictive
stories.description: The ruling ends the criminal case against the Maryland man whose wrongful deportation to El Salvador's CECOT prison reached the Supreme Court.

stories.title: Lutnick gave $5M to House GOP weeks before Epstein testimony
stories.description: An FEC filing made public Thursday shows the commerce secretary donated to the Congressional Leadership Fund on April 1, a month before his closed-door interview.

stories.title: US consumer sentiment hits all-time low in final May reading
stories.description: The University of Michigan index fell to 44.8, far below the preliminary 48.2 estimate, as rising gas prices and inflation fears weighed on households.

stories.title: All 'Broadview Six' charges dropped after grand jury misconduct
stories.description: A federal judge dismissed all remaining cases against the ICE protest defendants with prejudice after a closed-door hearing revealed prosecutorial errors.

stories.title: U.S. has 'almost completely excluded' Israel from Iran talks, officials say
stories.description: Israeli defense officials told The New York Times that Washington sidelined Israel from negotiations, deepening the Trump-Netanyahu rift.

stories.title: Russia developing seabed nuclear missiles, German investigation finds
stories.description: NATO is monitoring a secret Russian program to hide remote-controlled nuclear missiles on the Arctic Ocean floor, as Moscow wraps up its largest nuclear drills in years.

top.title: Trump confirms calling Netanyahu 'crazy' in call over Lebanon strikes
top.description: The president said he was "a little bit perturbed" by Israel's operations against Hezbollah, which he says are undermining U.S.-Iran peace talks.

top.title: CENTCOM calls Iran's claim of striking US destroyer a lie
top.description: Iran said it hit a US Navy destroyer's command center in the Gulf of Oman; CENTCOM said the claim is false.

top.title: Kim Jong Un calls for 'exponential' nuclear expansion
top.description: North Korea's leader toured a newly operational nuclear material production facility, saying output capacity has more than doubled in five years.

top.title: Israel, Lebanon agree to ceasefire contingent on Hezbollah withdrawal
top.description: The US-brokered deal, reached after two days of talks in Washington, calls for pilot zones where Lebanese forces would replace Hezbollah in the south.

top.title: Trump strips job protections from 8,000 federal workers
top.description: An executive order signed Wednesday converts senior career employees to at-will status, removing their right to appeal disciplinary actions or terminations.

top.title: Iran, US working on 'final formula' to end war, Araghchi says
top.description: Iran's foreign minister said both sides are reviewing exchanged texts, though he cautioned that no progress has been made in formal negotiations.

top.title: Germany, France, UK sketch plan to engage Putin on Ukraine
top.description: The three European allies see a shift in momentum favoring Kyiv as U.S.-mediated talks remain stalled since February.

top.title: House passes war powers resolution to end Iran war
top.description: Four Republicans joined Democrats in the 215-208 vote, delivering a bipartisan rebuke to President Trump over the three-month-old conflict.

top.title: Rubio says Greenland is part of Denmark 'for now'
top.description: The secretary of state told lawmakers that U.S. talks with Denmark and Greenland on collective defense are "in a good place."

top.title: Proactiv, Dr. Pimple Popper acne creams had benzene 15 times FDA limit
top.description: Bloomberg reported that government lab results showed recalled acne products from Proactiv and SLMD contained benzene levels far exceeding federal safety limits.

top.title: Pentagon watchdog to lead oversight of Iran war
top.description: Inspector General Platte B. Moring III will coordinate reviews across the Pentagon, State Department, and USAID for Operation Epic Fury.

top.title: Trump confirms he will attend G7 summit in France
top.description: The president said on Truth Social he will travel to Évian-les-Bains after a UFC fight at the White House on June 14, his 80th birthday.

top.title: Bass, Pratt advance to LA mayoral runoff after Tuesday primary
top.description: Early results show the incumbent mayor leading with roughly 37%, followed by reality TV star Spencer Pratt, who edged out Nithya Raman for second place.

top.title: Vanguard's VOO becomes first ETF to hit $1 trillion
top.description: The S&P 500 tracker crossed the milestone after averaging more than $1 billion in daily inflows, according to the Financial Times.

top.title: Trump administration appeals order to refund $166B in tariffs
top.description: The DOJ argues the trade court cannot mandate universal refunds, potentially forcing importers who haven't filed individual lawsuits to sue for their money back.

top.title: California primary sets November matchups across key races
top.description: Wiener and Chan will compete for Pelosi's former House seat, while Bass heads to an L.A. mayoral runoff and the governor's race remains too close to call.

top.title: Ukraine hits St. Petersburg oil terminal as Putin's economic forum opens
top.description: Zelenskyy confirmed overnight drone strikes on the oil terminal, a naval base, and a weapons facility, targeting sites over 1,100 kilometers from Ukraine's border.

top.title: Trump says he and Iran's Khamenei 'getting along quite well,' eyes meeting
top.description: The president reiterated that Tehran agreed to forgo nuclear weapons and said a face-to-face meeting with the supreme leader is likely.

top.title: Iran drone strike hits Kuwait airport, halting flights
top.description: Iranian drones and missiles struck Terminal 1 at Kuwait International Airport on Wednesday, injuring several and forcing a full suspension of commercial flights.

top.title: US proposes tariffs on 60 economies over forced labor
top.description: The sweeping Section 301 action covers more than 99% of U.S. imports and marks a major step in rebuilding tariff authority after the Supreme Court struck down IEEPA levies.

top.title: Iowa farmer Lahn defeats Trump-backed Feenstra in GOP governor primary
top.description: Zach Lahn, aligned with RFK Jr.'s MAHA movement, edged Rep. Randy Feenstra by less than a point in Tuesday's five-candidate race.

top.title: SpaceX plans to price record IPO at $135 a share
top.description: The rocket and satellite company aims to raise $75 billion in the largest public offering ever, more than doubling Saudi Aramco's record.

top.title: Bolivia's defense minister resigns amid weeks of mass protests
top.description: Marcelo Salinas's departure is the highest-level exit from President Rodrigo Paz's government since unrest erupted in early May.

top.title: Trump calls Vance-Rubio 2028 ticket 'unbeatable'
top.description: The president's podcast remarks are his most direct praise yet of the pairing, though he still stopped short of a formal endorsement.

top.title: CBS fires Scott Pelley after clash with new '60 Minutes' boss
top.description: The veteran correspondent was terminated Tuesday after accusing Bari Weiss of "murdering" the iconic newsmagazine during a staff meeting.

top.title: Supreme Court lets Alabama eliminate a Black-majority district
top.description: The 6-3 ruling allows a GOP-drawn congressional map for the 2026 midterms, overriding a lower court finding of intentional racial discrimination.

top.title: Dr. Oz helms White House briefing, dodges non-health questions
top.description: The CMS administrator announced 160 new drugs on the TrumpRx discount platform while deflecting questions outside his portfolio.

top.title: Jeffries says Democrats not focused on Trump impeachment
top.description: The House minority leader told business leaders the party will prioritize affordability and anti-corruption ahead of the November midterms.

top.title: George Santos faces federal probe over Kalshi bets on his own SOTU attendance
top.description: The DOJ and CFTC are investigating whether the disgraced former congressman profited by wagering against his own appearance at the State of the Union.

top.title: Trump endorses Colombia presidential candidate De La Espriella
top.description: The endorsement came via Truth Social on Tuesday as the conservative outsider prepares to face leftist senator Ivan Cepeda in a June 21 runoff.

top.title: IRGC attacks US Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain
top.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards launched missile and drone attacks on the U.S. naval base and a regional airbase, escalating tit-for-tat exchanges.

top.title: Navy veteran Bennett wins NJ-7 primary to face absent Rep. Kean
top.description: Bennett will challenge Rep. Tom Kean Jr., who has missed over 100 congressional votes during a months-long undisclosed medical absence.

top.title: Senate confirms first 'not qualified' judge of Trump's second term
top.description: Katie Lane was approved 52-46 for a Montana federal judgeship despite the ABA finding she had never tried a case as lead counsel.

top.title: Rubio says Netanyahu's plan to seize 70% of Gaza contradicts US vision
top.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio told lawmakers Tuesday that Israel's expansion directive is not part of Washington's plan for the territory.

top.title: UNICEF says Iran war delaying lifesaving aid for children
top.description: The agency warned Tuesday that surging transport costs and Gulf shipping disruptions are forcing it to rely on costly air freight to reach children.

top.title: Oz calls Trump's health 'spectacular' after latest checkup
top.description: The CMS administrator dismissed concerns about Trump's frequent Walter Reed visits, saying the president keeps returning because he "likes the results."

top.title: US sanctions two rebel commanders over eastern Congo conflict
top.description: The Treasury Department targeted an M23 intelligence chief and an FDLR special operations commander fueling violence in the DRC.

top.title: Kremlin demands Ukraine cede territory hours after deadly mass attack
top.description: Russia launched 73 missiles and 656 drones at Ukrainian cities, killing at least 22, before demanding Kyiv withdraw from occupied regions.

top.title: Pentagon hires convicted Jan. 6 rioter for counterterrorism role
top.description: Elias Irizarry, who pleaded guilty to entering the Capitol during the Jan. 6 attack, was appointed to the Defense Department's irregular warfare office.

top.title: Bahrain bans citizens from traveling to Iran and Iraq
top.description: The ban, citing security tensions, comes as millions of Shiites prepare for pilgrimage season, drawing accusations of sectarian discrimination.

top.title: Rubio says five countries may take stranded Afghan allies
top.description: More than 1,000 Afghans who aided U.S. forces remain stuck at a base in Qatar, but Rubio told senators they won't be resettled in America.

top.title: Platner meets with Senate Democrats amid sexting scandal
top.description: The Maine Democratic frontrunner visited Washington for fundraisers and face-to-face meetings as senators weigh his viability one week before the primary.

top.title: Blanche confirms end to $1.8B anti-weaponization amid backlash
top.description: Acting AG Todd Blanche told Congress the administration will not move forward with the controversial taxpayer-funded program.

top.title: Hegseth blocks Navy officers from admiral promotions
top.description: The defense secretary removed at least seven captains from a one-star admiral list, including two women and two Black men, reports say.

top.title: Takaichi urges Iran to restore free passage through Hormuz
top.description: Japan's PM called on Iran's president to show flexibility in U.S.-Iran talks and reopen the strait to all vessels.

top.title: Trump audit immunity remains unchallenged after anti-weaponization fund pause
top.description: The audit shield blocking IRS probes of Trump's past tax filings faces no direct legal challenge, even as a linked $1.8 billion fund is frozen.

top.title: Hundreds protest US Ebola quarantine facility in Kenya
top.description: Demonstrators in Nanyuki demanded the closure of a planned 50-bed facility at Laikipia Air Base days after a High Court order halted the project.

top.title: Russia launches major strike on Kyiv, setting apartment buildings ablaze
top.description: Moscow followed through on its threat of "systematic strikes," hitting a 24-story residential building and sparking fires across Ukraine's capital early Tuesday.

top.title: White House Correspondents' Dinner set for July 24 after shooting postponement
top.description: The WHCA announced the rescheduled date Tuesday, nearly three months after a gunman disrupted the original April 25 event at the Washington Hilton.

top.title: State Dept. to cut Africa visa-processing sites from 50 to 20 hubs
top.description: Secretary of State Rubio approved a directive consolidating consular operations across Africa, forcing applicants in non-hub countries to travel abroad for visas.

top.title: Frederiksen secures third term as Denmark's PM with new centre-left coalition
top.description: The deal ends over two months of deadlocked talks following March's fragmented snap election, which saw 12 parties win seats in parliament.

top.title: Trump signs scaled-back AI executive order with 30-day model review
top.description: The executive order asks AI companies to voluntarily submit powerful models to federal review before release, down from an earlier 90-day proposal.

top.title: Pentagon bars journalists from press office, declaring it classified space
top.description: The designation marks the latest escalation in a yearlong campaign to restrict media access to the Defense Department.

top.title: EU climate chief says COP summits falling short of science
top.description: Wopke Hoekstra's remarks come a week before the Bonn SB64 conference, seen as a key test of multilateral climate commitments.

top.title: Former FBI officials launch support network for agents under Patel
top.description: The group offers legal, mental health, and career assistance to employees facing what organizers call politically motivated firings and intimidation.

top.title: Appeals court rules Pentagon transgender troop ban illegal
top.description: A divided D.C. Circuit panel ruled the Pentagon's transgender military ban illegal but allowed the military to keep barring new enlistees.

top.title: Iran cuts off U.S. talks, vows to fully block Hormuz
top.description: Tehran says it will completely shut the Strait of Hormuz and halt all negotiations until Israel withdraws from Lebanon, according to state media.

top.title: Anthropic confidentially files for IPO after $965B valuation
top.description: The Claude AI maker submitted a draft S-1 to the SEC on Monday, joining SpaceX and OpenAI in a historic wave of tech IPOs expected this year.

top.title: Florida sues OpenAI and Sam Altman over AI safety
top.description: The state becomes the first to sue OpenAI, alleging the company prioritized profit over safety in marketing ChatGPT to users.

top.title: Armenia's Pashinyan rejects Russia's call for EU referendum
top.description: The Armenian prime minister pushed back on Monday as Moscow escalated pressure over Yerevan's ties with the European Union ahead of June 7 elections.

top.title: Nvidia unveils RTX Spark, its first Windows PC chip in over a decade
top.description: CEO Jensen Huang announced the Arm-based processor at Computex, calling it "the most efficient PC chip ever built."

top.title: CERN finds high-significance hint of physics beyond the Standard Model
top.description: Rare B meson decays measured at the LHC deviate from predictions at four sigma, giving physicists a one-in-16,000 chance of a statistical fluke.

top.title: Blast at explosives storage site kills dozens in Myanmar
top.description: At least 46 people died and about 70 were injured after an explosion at a mining explosives depot in Shan State on Sunday.

top.title: Trump tours ballroom site, says inauguration will be held there
top.description: During a Fox News interview with Lara Trump, the president revealed plans to host the next inauguration in the new White House ballroom.

top.title: France detains 780 after deadly riots follow PSG's Champions League win
top.description: One person was found dead and 219 injured as celebrations of PSG's second straight title devolved into mass disorder across 15 French cities.

top.title: Taiwan vows not to be 'silenced' after China expels NYT reporter
top.description: The presidential office condemned Beijing after China expelled a New York Times correspondent over a Taiwan president interview.

top.title: Trump signs order to narrow childhood vaccine schedule
top.description: The executive order directs the CDC to align with an HHS assessment recommending vaccines for 11 diseases instead of the current 17.

top.title: Hegseth denies Iran war delayed $14B Taiwan arms sale
top.description: Speaking at the Shangri-La Dialogue, the defense secretary contradicted a Navy official who said the deal was paused to conserve munitions.

top.title: Lula rejects U.S. terrorist label for Brazil's largest gangs
top.description: Brazil's president called the designation "disappointing" and accused Washington of treating Brazil "like a banana republic."

top.title: Philippines says China threat persists despite Trump-Xi summit
top.description: Defense Secretary Teodoro said at the Shangri-La Dialogue that the recent U.S.-China diplomatic thaw has not eased South China Sea tensions.

top.title: Hegseth says China 'credibly preparing' for military action in Asia
top.description: The defense secretary spoke Saturday at the Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore as China's defense minister skipped the summit for a second year.

top.title: Quarter of trapped oil tankers slip out of Hormuz
top.description: Twenty-nine of 109 large tankers stranded since the Iran war began have crossed the strait, but Chevron's CEO says vessels are still being attacked.

top.title: Federal judge presses DOJ to justify $100,000 H-1B fee
top.description: Judge Leo Sorokin questioned the limits of presidential power to impose the fee, posing pointed hypotheticals to DOJ lawyers during a Friday hearing.

top.title: Rubio thanks Pakistan for Iran mediation efforts
top.description: Secretary of State Rubio met with Pakistani Foreign Minister Dar on Friday, praising Islamabad's role in mediating the U.S.-Iran conflict.

top.title: Bondi acknowledges redaction errors in Epstein files testimony
top.description: The former attorney general defended DOJ's overall handling of the files but refused to apologize for failures that exposed survivors' identities.

top.title: 'Gang of Six' GOP senators threaten Trump's agenda
top.description: Six Republican senators have emerged as a bloc capable of derailing Trump's priorities in the narrowly divided Senate.

top.title: Top US general meets Cuban military leaders at Guantanamo Bay
top.description: Gen. Francis Donovan held rare talks with senior Cuban officers at the base perimeter Friday amid a sharp escalation in U.S.-Cuba tensions.

top.title: US-Iran draft deal includes $300B reconstruction fund for Tehran
top.description: Gulf states and China would finance the program, contingent on a final peace agreement ending the three-month war.

top.title: Judge reopens Trump's $10B IRS lawsuit over fraud claims
top.description: U.S. District Judge Kathleen Williams ordered Trump's legal team to address allegations that the settlement with DOJ was "premised on deception."

top.title: China building 80-plus launch pads near nuclear silos
top.description: Reuters satellite imagery reveals a sprawling network of launch stations, bunkers, and communications hubs in Xinjiang designed to bolster Beijing's second-strike capability.

top.title: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket explodes during test at Cape Canaveral
top.description: The blast Thursday evening shook nearby homes but caused no injuries, dealing a fresh blow to Jeff Bezos' rocket program weeks before a planned launch.

top.title: Judge strikes down New Hampshire's proof-of-citizenship voting law
top.description: The ruling immediately restores sworn affidavits as an option for new voters who lack a birth certificate or passport to prove citizenship.

top.title: Bessent signals phased Iran sanctions relief tied to deal benchmarks
top.description: Trump weighs a tentative 60-day agreement as the Treasury secretary says any rollback would come slowly and only after Tehran meets key demands.

top.title: Hungary's Magyar vows 'reliable' NATO role but won't arm Ukraine
top.description: Prime Minister Magyar met NATO chief Rutte in Brussels to discuss the Ankara summit and pledged renewed alliance cooperation.

top.title: Japan calls Russian criticism of its military buildup 'ridiculous' at UN
top.description: Tokyo's UN ambassador pushed back after Russia accused Japan and Germany of dangerous remilitarization at a Security Council debate.

top.title: Democrats threaten subpoenas after Bondi dodges Trump questions in Epstein hearing
top.description: Bondi acknowledged redaction errors but refused to discuss Trump, shifting responsibility to Acting AG Todd Blanche.

top.title: US denies Iran's claim it destroyed American aircraft in Bushehr
top.description: The unverified assertion, broadcast on Iranian state TV, comes as both sides reached a preliminary deal to extend their ceasefire by 60 days.

top.title: Hochul signs NYC pied-à-terre tax into law as part of $269B budget
top.description: The new surcharge on luxury second homes, set to take effect July 1, is expected to generate $500 million annually for New York City.

top.title: Talarico says he raised $3M in a day after Paxton's Texas runoff win
top.description: The Democratic Senate nominee's record haul highlights party enthusiasm for flipping a Texas seat held by Republicans since 1993.

top.title: Bari Weiss shakes up '60 Minutes' with new producer, ousted correspondents
top.description: CBS News editor-in-chief replaced the executive producer and dismissed multiple correspondents in one of the program's most dramatic restructurings.

top.title: Supreme Court sides with Black death row inmate on jury bias
top.description: In a 5-4 ruling, the justices found Mississippi courts wrongly concluded Terry Pitchford waived his right to challenge racial discrimination in jury selection.

top.title: Whitmer says she won't run for president in 2028
top.description: The Michigan governor ended months of speculation at the Mackinac Policy Conference, saying a "robust group" will run but she won't be among them.

top.title: Israel strikes Beirut suburb targeting Iranian militia missile commander
top.description: The Choueifat attack, the first near the capital in weeks, came one day before new Pentagon security talks between Israel and Lebanon.

top.title: NYC Mayor Mamdani launches charter Commission on Government Efficiency
top.description: The Commission on Government Efficiency will review the city charter and bring proposed amendments to voters in November.

top.title: Trump refiles $10B defamation suit against WSJ over Epstein report
top.description: The amended complaint, filed Tuesday in Miami federal court, names Rupert Murdoch, News Corp, and two Journal reporters as defendants.

top.title: April PCE inflation hits 3-year high as Fed's Warsh faces first price test
top.description: The headline PCE index rose 3.8% year-over-year while core PCE came in at 3.3%, marking the steepest inflation since 2023.

top.title: Tesla's Optimus robot factory raises first steel at Giga Texas
top.description: Drone footage shows vertical steel framing at the Austin site designed for up to 10 million humanoid robots per year.

top.title: Judge declines to block Trump mail-in voting order
top.description: Democrats' challenge was premature because no agency has acted on the directive yet, the court ruled.

top.title: US sanctions Iran's Hormuz toll authority, cites IRGC ties
top.description: Treasury designated the Persian Gulf Strait Authority, the body collecting transit fees from ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz.

top.title: Ex-CIA officer charged after FBI finds 303 gold bars in his home
top.description: David Rush allegedly checked out tens of millions in gold from the CIA for "work expenses" and kept it, authorities say.

top.title: Trump administration in talks to fund US drone companies
top.description: Unusual Machines, linked to Donald Trump Jr., and other manufacturers are in negotiations with the Pentagon for production financing, the Wall Street Journal reported.

top.title: Israel declares 2,000 sq km of southern Lebanon a combat zone
top.description: The IDF warned residents to evacuate north of the Zahrani River as it prepares expanded operations against Hezbollah despite an April ceasefire.

top.title: US re-adds UN expert Albanese to sanctions list
top.description: The Treasury Department restored the designation Wednesday after an appeals court stayed a lower court order that had blocked the measures.

top.title: Park entrance fees fund Trump's D.C. projects, Times finds
top.description: At least $67 million in national park visitor fees is being spent on fountains and the Reflecting Pool renovation in Washington.

top.title: EU sets June 16 date to open Ukraine accession talks
top.description: The European Commission plans to propose opening the first cluster of membership negotiations with Ukraine and Moldova, ending a two-year deadlock.

top.title: DHS Secretary Mullin dismisses Newark hunger strike, blames Kim for pepper-spray clash
top.description: Mullin called the protest a dispute over "ethnic food" at a Cabinet meeting, as Sen. Booker visited Delaney Hall and called it "a moral stain."

top.title: Trump says Iran 'negotiating on fumes,' midterms won't alter war strategy
top.description: During a Cabinet meeting, the president insisted electoral pressures would not soften his stance as peace talks with Tehran remain uncertain.

top.title: US needs years to rebuild weapons used in Iran war, CSIS finds
top.description: A new analysis warns that restocking Tomahawk missiles, Patriot interceptors, and THAAD interceptors could take until 2030, raising concerns about readiness against China.

top.title: North Korea claims first use of AI in missile tests
top.description: Kim Jong Un supervised Tuesday's tests of AI-guided cruise missiles, nuclear-capable ballistic warheads, and precision artillery rockets, KCNA reported.

top.title: Biden sues DOJ to block release of ghostwriter audio
top.description: The Trump DOJ reversed its position and plans to release roughly 70 hours of recordings to Congress and the Heritage Foundation by June 15.

top.title: Cuba calls US oil blockade 'an act of war' at UN Security Council
top.description: Foreign Minister Bruno Rodríguez Parrilla urged the international community to mobilize against what he called an unfolding humanitarian catastrophe.

top.title: UN Security Council condemns drone strike on UAE nuclear plant
top.description: The council unanimously called the May 17 attack on Barakah a violation of international law but did not assign blame.

top.title: Trump's Board of Peace fund sits empty despite $17B in pledges
top.description: The World Bank-managed reconstruction fund for Gaza has received no donor money four months after launch, the Financial Times reported.

top.title: DOJ sues UCLA over antisemitism in third suit against UC this year
top.description: The lawsuit alleges the university was "deliberately indifferent" to harassment of Jewish and Israeli students during a 2024 pro-Palestinian encampment.

top.title: Iran's judiciary blocks presidential order to restore internet
top.description: President Pezeshkian ordered international internet access restored after a near-90-day blackout, but the judiciary suspended the body tasked with carrying it out.

top.title: Trump moves Cabinet meeting to White House as Iran deal hangs in balance
top.description: The president cited weather concerns for shifting a rare full Cabinet retreat from Camp David, with fragile US-Iran negotiations set to dominate the agenda.

top.title: Supreme Court rejects Florida's bid to sue over immigrant truck licenses
top.description: The justices declined to hear the state-versus-state dispute over commercial driver's licenses issued to undocumented immigrants.

top.title: Zelensky says US missile defense talks have stalled
top.description: Ukraine's president declared air defense Kyiv's top priority and said he is accelerating efforts to produce anti-ballistic systems in Europe instead.

top.title: SPLC asks judge to toss DOJ indictment, alleging vindictive prosecution
top.description: Lawyers argue the 11-count case is the "culmination of a top-down, retributive campaign" directed by President Trump against political enemies.

top.title: Cuba publishes names of 2,000 pardoned prisoners
top.description: The full list, released Monday in the official gazette, enables rights groups to verify whether any political detainees were included in the April amnesty.

top.title: Putin signs decree canceling debts for new Ukraine war recruits
top.description: The Kremlin is offering up to 10 million roubles in debt relief to those who enlist for at least one year, along with their spouses.

top.title: Rubio aide Needham promoted to deputy national security adviser
top.description: The move deepens Rubio's hold on national security policy as he continues serving as both secretary of state and acting NSA.

top.title: Iran says Khamenei suffered only 'superficial' wounds in February airstrikes
top.description: Health Ministry officials described the supreme leader's injuries as minor, contradicting months of reports that he was in a coma or lost a leg.

top.title: Carney calls Alberta separation vote a 'dangerous bluff,' cites Brexit experience
top.description: The prime minister drew on his time leading the Bank of England during Brexit to warn Albertans against treating the October referendum as leverage.

top.title: South Carolina Senate kills Trump-backed redistricting plan
top.description: Record early voting turnout helped sway Republicans to reject new congressional maps targeting Rep. Jim Clyburn's district.

top.title: Massie files for 2028 race days after Trump-backed primary defeat
top.description: The Kentucky congressman declined to rule out a presidential bid, saying he hasn't decided which office to seek.

top.title: AI safety filters on Meta, Google models stripped in minutes, FT finds
top.description: Freely available software tools can remove guardrails from major AI models, enabling responses on bioweapons, malware, and exploitation.

top.title: Trump administration proposes NDAs for all federal workers
top.description: OPM published a draft nondisclosure agreement in the Federal Register on Tuesday, targeting leaks of internal government information to the media.

top.title: Pope Leo XIV calls to 'disarm' AI in first encyclical
top.description: "Magnifica Humanitas" warns against an unchecked technology race and declares just war theory "outdated" in the age of autonomous weapons.

top.title: Oil and LNG tankers resume Strait of Hormuz transits after three-month halt
top.description: Limited commercial shipping is restarting through the critical chokepoint, though volumes remain far below pre-war levels.

top.title: Supreme Court backs Trump on immigration judge speech curbs
top.description: The justices reversed a Fourth Circuit ruling on procedural grounds without addressing whether the speech restrictions are constitutional.

top.title: Inaudible sounds hidden in podcasts can hijack AI voice assistants, researchers find
top.description: Researchers demonstrated that imperceptible audio embedded in everyday media can trick voice AI into unauthorized actions like sending emails or downloading files.

top.title: NATO weighs return to Iraq after wartime evacuation
top.description: The alliance's advisory mission was fully relocated to Italy in March after Iranian strikes on bases in northern Iraq.

top.title: DOJ cites White House shooting to push for ballroom project
top.description: The Justice Department filed a brief Sunday invoking Saturday's fatal shooting near the White House to argue for lifting the injunction on Trump's $400 million ballroom.

top.title: Iranian hackers posed as recruiters to target US aviation engineers
top.description: Palo Alto Networks' Unit 42 uncovered a campaign using fake job ads and compromised video tools to infiltrate aviation and energy networks.

top.title: US naval blockade of Iran hits 100 redirected ships
top.description: CENTCOM announced Friday that forces have redirected more than 100 commercial vessels since the blockade of Iranian ports began April 13.

top.title: Trump urges Arab leaders to normalize Israel ties as Iran deal nears
top.description: Arab and Muslim leaders were silent when Trump pressed them to join the Abraham Accords during a call about the pending Iran deal.

top.title: Zelensky confirms strike on chemical plant 1,700 km inside Russia
top.description: Ukraine's overnight drone campaign hit a Perm Krai chemical plant and a Novorossiysk oil terminal, with Zelensky saying production has been "completely halted."

top.title: Zelensky calls Germany's associate EU membership plan 'unfair'
top.description: Ukraine's president told EU leaders the proposal would leave Kyiv "voiceless" and urged full membership instead.

top.title: Taiwan says China deployed 100-plus vessels after Trump-Xi summit
top.description: The fleet spans from the Yellow Sea to the South China Sea and western Pacific, marking one of Beijing's largest maritime shows of force in years.

top.title: Democrats demand Rubio explain visa for fugitive Polish minister
top.description: House Democrats called the expedited visa for wanted ex-minister Zbigniew Ziobro "a massive abuse of power" and demanded answers by June 21.

top.title: SpaceX launches first Starship V3 but loses booster over Gulf of Mexico
top.description: The 12th test flight debuted SpaceX's most powerful rocket design, but engine failures sent the Super Heavy booster crashing into the Gulf of Mexico.

top.title: DOJ deletes press releases on Jan. 6 charges from its website
top.description: NBC News found the vast majority of news releases about Jan. 6 defendants were removed, as a $1.776 billion compensation fund draws bipartisan scrutiny.

top.title: Iran condemns US sanctions on its envoy to Lebanon
top.description: Tehran called the Treasury Department's designation of ambassador Mohammad Reza Sheibani "illegal," accusing Washington of violating international law.

top.title: Judge tosses Abrego Garcia smuggling charges as vindictive
top.description: The ruling ends the criminal case against the Maryland man whose wrongful deportation to El Salvador's CECOT prison reached the Supreme Court.

top.title: Lutnick gave $5M to House GOP weeks before Epstein testimony
top.description: An FEC filing made public Thursday shows the commerce secretary donated to the Congressional Leadership Fund on April 1, a month before his closed-door interview.

top.title: US consumer sentiment hits all-time low in final May reading
top.description: The University of Michigan index fell to 44.8, far below the preliminary 48.2 estimate, as rising gas prices and inflation fears weighed on households.

top.title: All 'Broadview Six' charges dropped after grand jury misconduct
top.description: A federal judge dismissed all remaining cases against the ICE protest defendants with prejudice after a closed-door hearing revealed prosecutorial errors.

top.title: U.S. has 'almost completely excluded' Israel from Iran talks, officials say
top.description: Israeli defense officials told The New York Times that Washington sidelined Israel from negotiations, deepening the Trump-Netanyahu rift.

top.title: Russia developing seabed nuclear missiles, German investigation finds
top.description: NATO is monitoring a secret Russian program to hide remote-controlled nuclear missiles on the Arctic Ocean floor, as Moscow wraps up its largest nuclear drills in years.
